2603.17333.title: Grid Spatial Understanding: A Dataset for Textual Spatial Reasoning over Grids, Embodied Settings, and Coordinate Structures
2603.17333.paper_summary: We introduce GSU, a text-only grid dataset to evaluate the spatial reasoning capabilities of LLMs over 3 core tasks: navigation, object localization, and structure composition. By forgoing visual inputs, isolating spatial reasoning from perception, we show that while most models grasp basic grid concepts, they struggle with frames of reference relative to an embodied agent and identifying 3D shapes from coordinate lists. We also find that exposure to a visual modality does not provide a generalizable understanding of 3D space that VLMs are able to utilize for these tasks. Finally, we show that while the very latest frontier models can solve the provided tasks (though harder variants may still stump them), fully fine-tuning a small LM or LORA fine-tuning a small LLM show potential to match frontier model performance, suggesting an avenue for specialized embodied agents.
2603.17333.paper_summary_zh: 我們介紹 GSU，這是一個僅包含文本的網格數據集，用於評估 LLM 在三個核心任務上的空間推理能力：導航、物體定位和結構組合。通過放棄視覺輸入，將空間推理與感知隔離，我們顯示出雖然大多數模型掌握了基本的網格概念，但它們在相對於具身代理的參考框架以及從坐標列表中識別 3D 形狀方面存在困難。我們還發現，接觸視覺模態並未提供 VLM 能夠用於這些任務的 3D 空間的可泛化理解。最後，我們展示了雖然最新的前沿模型能夠解決提供的任務（儘管更難的變體仍可能讓它們感到困惑），但對小型 LM 進行完全微調或對小型 LLM 進行 LORA 微調顯示出與前沿模型性能相匹配的潛力，這暗示了專門具身代理的一條途徑。
2603.17333.author: Risham Sidhu et.al.
